metadata.summary: Dispatcher index for the software-developer skill. Maps task keywords to canonical sibling methodology folders. Not a standalone methodology — every entry is a routing pointer, not a spec.

content.title: Software Developer Methodology Index
content.summary: A dispatcher index for the software-developer skill. Maps task keywords to the canonical sibling methodology folder under free/dev/software-developer/. Each sibling contains the full, authoritative content; this index gives the agent a starting routing point only. Never implement directly from this index — every entry is a routing pointer, not a specification.

why.p: The legacy umbrella README listed 68 patterns as 5-line stubs, causing agents to produce shallow output by quoting stubs instead of loading the real sibling content. This index encodes the routing table and explicitly forbids agents from implementing directly from it.

list.item: Routing a coarse task ("implement a Django service", "scaffold a Go API") to the correct sibling folder.
Building a multi-step plan that requires knowing the canonical order: Python → JS/TS → backend → DevOps → docs.
Auditing which methodology slugs exist before dispatching parallel subagents.
Implementing from this file directly — every entry is a routing pointer, not a spec.
Treating it as authoritative best-practices guidance — pull current guidance from each sibling.
Citing it as the source of any rule — cite the sibling folder instead.

rule.statement: Always hop to the sibling folder. This index is a map, not a manual. Load the sibling AGENTS.md before implementing anything.
rule.rationale: The stub code in this index is 5-line skeletons. Agents that quote them produce shallow output. The full authoritative content lives one folder away.

rule.statement: Validate agent names against the actual agents/ directory before delegating. Do not use names from historical rows in this index.
rule.rationale: Historical agent names (faion-code-agent, faion-test-agent, faion-devops-agent) do not exist. Only faion-sdd-executor-agent, nero-sdd-executor-agent, and password-scrubber-agent are present.

list.item: Python / Django patterns → django-coding-standards, django-testing
Python modern features (3.12/3.13), async, type hints → best-practices-2026
Go concurrency, goroutines, worker pools → go-concurrency
Test-first development (any language) → tdd-workflow
Storybook, component documentation, visual testing → storybook-setup
CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md creation or audit → documentation
2026 best practices across TS/React/Python/AI → best-practices-2026
DevOps, Docker, K8s, Terraform, CI/CD → pro/infra/ siblings (out of scope here)

section.name: canonical-ordering-for-cross-cutting-tasks
section.p: When a task spans multiple domains, apply siblings in this order: Python ecosystem → JS/TS → other backends → DevOps → docs. This is a sequencing guide, not a priority ranking.

section.name: agent-gotchas
section.p: Known ways agents misuse this index and how to prevent them.

antipattern.description: Stub seduction: agent reads the 5-line code samples in this index and treats them as complete implementations, producing shallow output without loading sibling content.
antipattern.reason: This index was intentionally kept as stubs. Implementing from stubs is guaranteed to miss edge cases, error handling, and project conventions documented in siblings.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated agent names: agent calls Task(subagent_type="faion-code-agent", ...) or similar because the index lists that name.
antipattern.reason: These agent names are aspirational. The call fails at runtime. Only faion-sdd-executor-agent, nero-sdd-executor-agent, and password-scrubber-agent exist.

antipattern.description: Outdated framework references: agent quotes the generic "Next.js App Router" or "React Component Pattern" snippets from the legacy README and ships 2-year-old patterns.
antipattern.reason: Current guidance is in best-practices-2026. The legacy index predates React 19, TypeScript 5, and Python 3.12.

antipattern.description: Section count drift: agent trusts the headline "68 methodologies" figure and assumes it reflects the current sibling folder count.
antipattern.reason: Use ls to count sibling folders; the headline figure drifts as new methodologies are added or removed.

antipattern.description: DevOps scope bleed: agent routes Kubernetes / Terraform work through this software-developer index instead of pro/infra/ siblings.
antipattern.reason: DevOps patterns here predate the pro/infra/* skill expansion and are stub-quality. Use the correct tier.

list.item: Always hop to the sibling. This index is a map, not a manual. The depth is one folder away.
Verify the agent table before delegating. The historical "Agents" rows reference names that may not exist; check agents/ and skills/*/agents/ first.
Use it for ordering, not content. The Python → JS → backend → DevOps → docs sequence is a sensible default for full-stack scaffolding tasks.
Pair with best-practices-2026/. When this index says "Python Type Hints," cross-read the 2026 file for current syntax.
Keep it as the routing layer. Resist the urge to expand it back into a textbook — fragmentation into siblings was the right call.
Audit quarterly. Run the stub-detector; sibling READMEs less than 80 lines are likely candidates for the next research batch.
Mark deprecations explicitly. When a methodology is superseded, edit this index to point to the replacement.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Use this index purely as a dispatcher. The recommended Claude flow: (1) parse the user's task; (2) match keywords against the section index; (3) read the sibling folder's AGENTS.md and agent-integration.md (where it exists) — those are the real source of truth; (4) execute under the SDD lifecycle using faion-sdd-executor-agent. Never quote stub snippets from this index in production code; always hop to the sibling.

Dispatch pass: Given the user task, consult the routing table and return (1) top 3 candidate sibling slugs, (2) for each, confidence (H/M/L) and one-line rationale, (3) recommended order if more than one applies, (4) flag any candidate whose sibling folder has only stub content. Do NOT implement; only route.

Read-the-real-doc pass: For the resolved sibling slug, load the full content from the sibling folder. Then proceed under the SDD lifecycle. Reject any plan that quotes this index's stubs instead of the sibling content.
